---
title: "BIOST 2155"
subtitle: "Introductory Statistical Learning for Health Sciences"
toc: false
---

::: {.lead}
A graduate-level introduction to statistical learning methods, with applications drawn from
modern health science data. Topics include linear regression with regularization,
classification, clustering, dimensionality reduction, and ensemble methods.
:::

## Course at a glance

|             |                                                          |
|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| **Term**    | Fall 2026                                                |
| **Credits** | 2                                                        |
| **Lecture** | Fridays 9:00–10:50 AM, A622 Public Health                |
| **Office hours** | Fridays 12:30–1:30 PM, A740 Public Health *(or by appointment)* |
| **Instructor** | Soumik Purkayastha · [soumik\@pitt.edu](mailto:soumik@pitt.edu) |

## Where to start

- The [schedule](schedule.qmd) lists each week with slides, readings, and code.
- The [assignments page](assignments/index.qmd) has the three homeworks.
- The [project page](project.qmd) describes the final project.
- The [syllabus](syllabus.qmd) covers grading, policies, and prerequisites.

## Recommended textbook

James, Witten, Hastie & Tibshirani. *An Introduction to Statistical Learning: with Applications in R.* Springer, 2013.
[Free PDF](https://www.statlearning.com/)

## Optional references

- Hastie–Tibshirani–Friedman *Elements of Statistical Learning*
- Bishop *Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning*
- Prince *Understanding Deep Learning*.
